harnessProfile.description: Root instruction surface plus current state, feature state, and compact harness README for the external pytm repair.
harnessProfile.name: moderate

limitations: External public repo scratch copy from OWASP pytm commit e452aaf, not an upstream issue or pull request.
Seeded source regression removed helper response linking, so the task is not benchmark-held-out.
Human review verified raw file_change events were limited to pytm/flows.py and focused post-run verification passed with 4 tests.
Repairs varied slightly in implementation shape while preserving the helper API and passing the focused behavior contract.
Codex JSONL did not emit the configured model name in this run.
Prompt/cache state from nearby runs may affect cached input buckets.
Raw JSONL contains local scratch paths and remains ignored under .harnessforge/.

mechanism.name: verification_feedback
mechanism.notes: External pytm scratch repair used focused pytest feedback first, profile-specific startup routing, per-run scratch HOME and CODEX_HOME, ignore-user-config, ignore-rules, disabled hooks/plugins/memories/apps/multi_agent, and workspace-write sandboxing.
mechanism.risk: Richer startup guidance can add context reads without improving a focused source repair.

task.description: Fix the failing pytm focused test in this external scratch copy. Follow the repository startup path for the current profile. Start by running `python -m pytest -q tests/test_flows_helpers.py` to observe the failure; this session PATH points `python` to the prepared scratch venv. Then make the smallest source-only Python change needed. Do not edit tests or docs unless the failure proves they are wrong. Rerun the same focused test. Do not commit or push. Final answer: one sentence with the changed file and verification result.
